# UC-13.5.14 · USE Method for Infrastructure (Utilization, Saturation, Errors)

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Intermediate &middot; **Pillar:** Observability &middot; **Type:** Performance, Capacity &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We check whether machines are actually full, backed up with waiting work, or reporting hardware faults—not just whether a website feels slow. That way we fix the real choke point instead of guessing.*

---

## Description

Operationalizes Brendan Gregg USE method (Utilization, Saturation, Errors) for every infrastructure resource class: CPU, memory, network interfaces, disks and controllers, I/O schedulers, kernel runqueues, GPUs, and hardware sensors—not for request-driven services, which belong to RED (UC-13.5.13). Utilization answers what fraction of time or capacity the resource was busy; saturation answers how much extra work is queued, delayed, swapped, throttled, or dropped beyond what utilization alone shows; errors answers corrected or uncorrected ECC, NIC framing drops, disk medium timeouts, GPU Xid resets, and hypervisor-visible device faults. The canonical checklist repeats those three questions per resource before declaring capacity innocence. UC-13.5.15 folds golden signals that mix RED plus USE; UC-13.5.16 focuses SLO burn; UC-13.5.20 tracks instrumentation coverage. This entry is the node, container, hypervisor, storage-array, and bare-metal resource lens: fleet dashboards and alerts derive the same vocabulary from node_exporter, cAdvisor, OpenTelemetry hostmetrics, kubeletstats, DCGM, cloud provider host metrics, and CIM Performance cross-checks so capacity reviews stay comparable across on-prem and multi-cloud.

## Value

Capacity and platform leaders stop debating whether a host is sick or merely busy: USE gives a repeatable worksheet so every incident drills utilization, saturation, and errors before blaming application code. Finance and engineering share one story when batch windows pin CPUs while queues remain shallow versus when queues explode while CPUs look idle. Hardware and datacenter partners receive evidence packets that separate cable-plant noise from NIC firmware defects, balloon-driven swap from true memory leaks, and RAID parity rebuild pressure from mis-sized pools. SREs integrate the same template with inventory-backed service levels so paging respects tier, role, GPU obligations, and deploy freezes instead of flat global thresholds. The pattern survives tool churn because Prometheus, OpenTelemetry, and cloud metric names map into one Splunk multisearch, preserving auditability when collectors change but the Gregg checklist does not.

## Implementation

Land node_exporter or OpenTelemetry hostmetrics into metrics indexes, cAdvisor and kubeletstats for pods, DCGM for GPUs, optional linux_secure or nix_syslog for MCE lines, and accelerated CIM Performance. Maintain host_inventory.csv with owner, tier, sla_cpu_util_pct, sla_mem_util_pct, sla_nic_drop_pct, deploy_freeze_until, golden_path_canary, gpu_required, role. Save obs_uc_13_5_14_use_infrastructure on a five-minute schedule earliest=-4h@h, route CRITICAL and HIGH to PagerDuty, and retune macros for your index names.

## Evidence

Saved search obs_uc_13_5_14_use_infrastructure with five-minute schedule; USE workbook dashboards; host_inventory.csv in lookup manager; weekly PDF exports to restricted index.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

In a lab host, run an approved CPU and memory stress harness until utilization exceeds twice sla_cpu_util_pct and saturation exceeds five times the trailing baseline while dmesg remains quiet; confirm obs_uc_13_5_14_use_infrastructure returns CRITICAL or HIGH with non-null recommended_action within ten minutes, then stop the harness and confirm severity falls.

### Negative scenario

After recovery on a steady-state host with utilization below SLA, saturation near its trailing baseline, and zero hardware error events in the window, confirm severity is LOW for non-canary rows and the alert wrapper returns zero rows when filtering to CRITICAL and HIGH only.

## Detailed Implementation

### Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control jointly with infrastructure capacity engineers, Kubernetes platform staff, hybrid cloud FinOps partners, and observability engineers who operate the Splunk Distribution of OpenTelemetry Collector fleet. The USE method, documented by Brendan Gregg for systems performance, names three measurements per resource: utilization as the fraction of time a resource was busy servicing work; saturation as the degree of queued or deferred work beyond what utilization alone reveals; and errors as explicit fault counters such as device errors, corrected or uncorrected memory events, or dropped packets. The method applies to CPUs, memory subsystems, network interfaces, storage devices and arrays, buses, GPUs, and hardware management controllers. It intentionally does not replace request-rate thinking for microservices; that is RED (Rate, Errors, Duration) in UC-13.5.13, which watches synchronous request paths rather than raw metal queues.

LETS (Latency, Errors, Traffic, Saturation) extends service thinking when queues dominate; USE stays closer to the scheduler and device counters. UC-13.5.15 composes golden signals that may blend RED and USE; UC-13.5.16 burns error and latency budgets against SLO windows; UC-13.5.20 audits whether traces and metrics exist at all. This UC is the infrastructure-resource checklist: node-level, container-level, hardware-level.

OpenTelemetry hostmetrics receiver lands system.cpu.utilization, system.memory.utilization, system.disk.io_time, system.network.drops, and system.network.errors with resource attributes for host.id and host.name. Pair it with kubeletstats when Kubernetes exposes pod-level pressure. node_exporter remains the Prometheus ecosystem workhorse for node_cpu_seconds_total, node_memory_MemAvailable_bytes, node_disk_io_time_seconds_total, node_network_receive_errs_total, and node_network_transmit_drop_total. cAdvisor exposes container_cpu_usage_seconds_total and container_memory_working_set_bytes for saturation relative to cgroup limits. NVIDIA DCGM exporters surface GPU utilization, memory copy saturation, and Xid error classes. VMware, AWS, Azure, and OCI host metrics replay the same story in cloud-native units once normalized.

Maintain host_inventory.csv with columns host, owner, team, tier, sla_cpu_util_pct, sla_mem_util_pct, sla_nic_drop_pct, deploy_freeze_until, golden_path_canary, gpu_required, role. Owners attest SLA columns quarterly. Deploy markers from change tooling stamp builds on parallel indexes or lookups so dashboards overlay releases. Alert tier policy encodes CRITICAL when utilization exceeds twice the CPU SLA while saturation exceeds five times the trailing runqueue or disk-queue baseline, or when any hardware error counter increments; HIGH when only one branch trips; MEDIUM on golden_path_canary hosts during controlled experiments; LOW during deploy_freeze_until windows parsed as dates.

RBAC restricts metrics and syslog indexes to platform identities. Legal review governs retention of hostnames tied to regulated data classes. Strip secrets from collector configs logged to internal indexes.

Differentiation recap: infrastructure USE via node_exporter, OTel hostmetrics, cAdvisor, DCGM, cloud host metrics, and Performance cross-check—not RED service request templates (UC-13.5.13), not composite golden-signal roll-ups alone (UC-13.5.15), not SLO burn math (UC-13.5.16), not coverage audits (UC-13.5.20).

Complementary ground-truth probes still matter for reviewers: iostat -x exposes await and queue depth that explain disk saturation when Prometheus scrape intervals smooth spikes; sar -n DEV highlights NIC errors and drops per interface; reading /proc/stat and /proc/meminfo on a jump host validates OTel hostmetrics during parser upgrades. IPMI sensor exports and Cisco UCS CIMC feeds contribute thermal and power-capacity context that pure CPU percentages omit. NetApp ONTAP and Dell PowerScale Isilon performance counters extend USE to array-side utilization and backend errors when application hosts look idle yet latency persists. Splunk Add-on for VMware maps vSphere host CPU ready, mem.swapused, and datastore latency into the same narrative. Splunk Add-on for AWS, Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Azure, and Splunk Add-on for Google Cloud Platform normalize EC2, Azure VM, and GCE instance metrics so multisearch arms stay portable across hybrid estates without rewriting thresholds per cloud.

Document expected dwell times for collector restarts so short gaps do not erase legitimate saturation peaks during exporter upgrades.

### Step 2 — Configure data collection

Lane one indexes Prometheus or OTLP metrics in _metrics or infra_metrics. mstats arms read node_exporter-style names and OpenTelemetry system.* names with span=5m. Map utilization to CPU busy percent, memory used percent, disk time percent, and NIC bandwidth proxies; map saturation to load averages, disk await queues, memory pressure stall counters, and transmit drops; map errors to disk read failures, NIC error counters, and OTel network.errors.

Lane two harvests cAdvisor kubernetes cadvisor endpoints or kubelet summary metrics. Container CPU throttling seconds and memory working set against limits signal saturation even when node CPU utilization looks moderate. coalesce pod, container, and instance labels into a host-equivalent key when you roll up to the node.

Lane three queries DCGM GPU utilization and Xid error metrics on gpu_required hosts from inventory. Normalize GPU utilization to zero on non-GPU roles so false positives do not page batch CPU estates.

Lane four runs tstats against the Performance data model for host.process cross-checks when legacy Unix add-ons land cpu_load_percent and mem_used_percent. This arm catches gaps where Prometheus is partial but Universal Forwarder still reports.

Lane five searches nix_syslog, linux_messages, or dmesg-style sourcetypes for Machine Check Exception, EDAC, I/O error, link reset, and NIC firmware strings. Parse counts into error increments for the closing case() statement.

After multisearch, each lane emits _time, host, use_lane, utilization, saturation, and errors fields on comparable scales. sort 0 by host and time stabilizes duplicates. streamstats time_window=5m builds sat_baseline as trailing saturation. join type=left enriches host_inventory.csv. eventstats perc95(utilization) AS tier_p95_util BY tier adds peer context. severity applies inventory SLA multiples. recommended_action emits terse tokens for runbooks.

Prefer mstats for high-volume five-minute rollups; fall back to metricstore or raw prometheus sourcetypes only in investigations. Document authoritative lanes per environment so analysts know whether OTel or node_exporter wins on conflict.

### Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as obs_uc_13_5_14_use_infrastructure with a five-minute schedule, dispatch earliest=-4h@h, dispatch latest=now, and alert when severity is CRITICAL or HIGH. Throttle duplicate host tuples for thirty minutes unless severity escalates. Wrap macro use_host(glob) if dashboards parameterize host sets.

Pipeline narrative: comment macro documents tunables. multisearch fans host mstats, cAdvisor container, DCGM GPU, Performance tstats, and dmesg hardware arms. coalesce unifies host, hostname, and node_name. streamstats time_window=5m tracks trailing saturation baselines. eventstats adds tier percentile context. join brings host_inventory.csv fields. case encodes CRITICAL when utilization breaches twice sla_cpu_util_pct while saturation exceeds five times the trailing baseline, or when errors exceed zero; HIGH when exactly one of the paired utilization or saturation breaches fires without the other and errors stay zero; MEDIUM for golden_path_canary; LOW for deploy freeze windows. recommended_action routes to vendor GPU logs, cable plant checks, or memory diagnostics.

Fenced SPL must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.5.14 USE infrastructure: Utilization-Saturation-Errors via multisearch host mstats, cAdvisor, DCGM, CIM Performance tstats, dmesg hardware hints. Tunables earliest=-4h@h span=5m. Join host_inventory.csv SLA columns.")`
| multisearch
    [ | mstats avg(_value) AS mv WHERE index IN ("_metrics","infra_metrics") earliest=-4h@h latest=now span=5m
        (metric_name="system.cpu.utilization" OR metric_name="system.cpu.time" OR metric_name="system.memory.utilization" OR metric_name="system.disk.io_time" OR metric_name="system.network.drops" OR metric_name="system.network.errors" OR like(metric_name,"node_cpu%") OR like(metric_name,"node_memory%") OR like(metric_name,"node_disk%") OR like(metric_name,"node_network%") OR like(metric_name,"node_load%"))
        BY _time metric_name host
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,hostname,node_name,""))))
      | eval utilization=if(match(metric_name,"cpu|load"), round(mv*100,3), if(match(metric_name,"memory|mem"), round(mv*100,3), if(match(metric_name,"disk|io_time"), round(mv*100,3), if(match(metric_name,"network"), round(mv*100,3), null()))))
      | eval saturation=if(match(metric_name,"load|queue|drops"), mv, if(match(metric_name,"io_time"), mv*10, null()))
      | eval errors=if(match(metric_name,"errors|err"), mv, 0)
      | eval use_lane="mstats_host"
      | fields _time host use_lane utilization saturation errors ]
    [ search index=infra_metrics OR index=k8s_metrics (sourcetype="prometheus:metrics" OR sourcetype="kube:metrics")
        ("container_cpu" OR "container_memory" OR "container_network" OR "cadvisor") earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,instance,pod,node,""))))
      | eval mn=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(metric_name,__name__,""))))
      | eval mv=tonumber(coalesce(value,_value,"0"))
      | eval utilization=if(match(mn,"cpu_usage"), round(min(mv*100,150),3), if(match(mn,"memory_working_set"), round(min(mv/1073741824*80,150),3), null()))
      | eval saturation=if(match(mn,"throttle"), mv, if(match(mn,"memory"), mv/1048576, null()))
      | eval errors=if(match(mn,"errors"), mv, 0)
      | bin _time span=5m
      | stats max(utilization) AS utilization max(saturation) AS saturation sum(errors) AS errors BY _time host
      | eval use_lane="cadvisor"
      | fields _time host use_lane utilization saturation errors ]
    [ search index=gpu_metrics OR index=_metrics ("DCGM" OR "dcgm" OR "nvidia_gpu") earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,Hostname,gpu_hostname,node,""))))
      | eval mn=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(__name__,metric_name,""))))
      | eval mv=tonumber(coalesce(value,_value,"0"))
      | eval utilization=if(match(mn,"util"), round(mv,3), null())
      | eval saturation=if(match(mn,"mem_copy"), mv, if(match(mn,"pcie"), mv, null()))
      | eval errors=if(match(mn,"xid|err"), mv, 0)
      | bin _time span=5m
      | stats max(utilization) AS utilization max(saturation) AS saturation sum(errors) AS errors BY _time host
      | eval use_lane="dcgm"
      | fields _time host use_lane utilization saturation errors ]
    [ | tstats avg(Performance.cpu_load_percent) AS cpu_ld avg(Performance.mem_used_percent) AS mem_u WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-4h@h latest=now BY _time span=5m Performance.host
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(Performance.host)))
      | eval utilization=round(max(cpu_ld,mem_u),3)
      | eval saturation=round(cpu_ld*1.2,3)
      | eval errors=0
      | eval use_lane="cim_performance"
      | fields _time host use_lane utilization saturation errors ]
    [ search index=os OR index=infra_logs (sourcetype="linux_messages" OR sourcetype="nix_syslog" OR sourcetype="dmesg")
        ("MCE" OR "Machine Check" OR "EDAC" OR "I/O error" OR "reset" OR "NIC" OR "nvme" OR "Xid") earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,hostname,dvc_host,""))))
      | bin _time span=5m
      | stats count AS err_evt BY _time host
      | eval utilization=0
      | eval saturation=0
      | eval errors=err_evt
      | eval use_lane="dmesg_hw"
      | fields _time host use_lane utilization saturation errors ]
| eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,hostname,node_name,""))))
| where len(host)>1
| bin _time span=5m
| stats max(utilization) AS utilization max(saturation) AS saturation sum(errors) AS errors values(use_lane) AS use_lanes BY _time host
| streamstats time_window=5m current=t global=f avg(saturation) AS sat_trail avg(utilization) AS util_trail BY host
| eval sat_baseline=coalesce(sat_trail,0.001)
| join type=left max=0 host
    [| inputlookup host_inventory.csv
     | eval host=lower(trim(toString(host)))
     | eval sla_cpu_util_pct=tonumber(sla_cpu_util_pct)
     | eval sla_mem_util_pct=tonumber(sla_mem_util_pct)
     | eval sla_nic_drop_pct=tonumber(sla_nic_drop_pct)
     | fields host owner team tier sla_cpu_util_pct sla_mem_util_pct sla_nic_drop_pct deploy_freeze_until golden_path_canary gpu_required role ]
| eval owner=coalesce(owner,"unowned")
| eval team=coalesce(team,"unknown_team")
| eval tier=coalesce(tier,"tier3")
| eval sla_cpu_util_pct=coalesce(sla_cpu_util_pct,85)
| eval sla_mem_util_pct=coalesce(sla_mem_util_pct,90)
| eval sla_nic_drop_pct=coalesce(sla_nic_drop_pct,0.1)
| eval deploy_epoch=if(len(deploy_freeze_until)>8,strptime(deploy_freeze_until,"%Y-%m-%d"),null())
| eval in_freeze=if(isnotnull(deploy_epoch) AND _time<=deploy_epoch,1,0)
| eval canary_flag=if(match(tostring(golden_path_canary),"^(1|true|yes)$"),1,0)
| eventstats perc95(utilization) AS tier_p95_util BY tier
| eval util_hi=if(utilization>(2*sla_cpu_util_pct),1,0)
| eval sat_hi=if(saturation>(5*sat_baseline),1,0)
| eval err_hi=if(errors>0,1,0)
| eval severity=case(
    canary_flag==1, "MEDIUM",
    in_freeze==1, "LOW",
    err_hi==1, "CRITICAL",
    util_hi==1 AND sat_hi==1, "CRITICAL",
    util_hi==1 OR sat_hi==1, "HIGH",
    true(), "LOW")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    err_hi==1, "triage_hw_logs_edac_nic_disk_and_vendor_case",
    util_hi==1 AND sat_hi==1, "capacity_add_scheduler_tune_and_io_path_review",
    util_hi==1, "cpu_mem_profile_and_right_size_workload",
    sat_hi==1, "inspect_queues_throttles_drops_and_network_path",
    true(), "observe_use_trend_checklist")
| table _time host owner team tier utilization saturation errors util_trail sat_trail sat_baseline tier_p95_util severity recommended_action use_lanes sla_cpu_util_pct
```

savedsearches.conf sketch:

```ini
[obs_uc_13_5_14_use_infrastructure_alert]
cron_schedule = */5 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -4h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch obs_uc_13_5_14_use_infrastructure | where severity IN ("CRITICAL","HIGH")
```

Performance: summarize five-minute host rollups into a summary index when Job Inspector queue time rises; keep this search for investigations.

### Step 4 — Validate

On a lab host approved for load testing, install stress-ng and run stress-ng --cpu 8 --vm 4 --vm-bytes 90% for ten minutes. Confirm utilization rises above twice sla_cpu_util_pct while saturation flags climb, observe CRITICAL or HIGH in obs_uc_13_5_14_use_infrastructure, then stop the test and confirm severity falls within two buckets. Restore baseline packages afterward.

Between two instrumented pods on the same cluster, run iperf3 UDP or TCP with parallel streams to elevate NIC drops or queue pressure on a constrained node. Confirm saturation arms move without matching utilization on all CPUs, validating that USE catches network bottlenecks RED misses.

On a disposable kernel lab with vendor approval, explore mce-inject or platform MCE simulation to raise corrected error counters; confirm the dmesg_hw arm increments errors and severity respects err_hi. Do not run destructive injections on production.

Cross-check mstats utilization against top, vmstat, and iostat on the same host during the window. Compare DCGM rows to nvidia-smi dmon on GPU nodes. Negative test: quiet fleet windows should yield LOW except canary rows.

Validate host_inventory.csv join keys with inputlookup alone; confirm tier-one hosts carry sla_cpu_util_pct. Document expected RAID rebuild signatures so validators do not file false defects during parity work.

### Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Publish a per-tier USE workbook dashboard: for each resource row, three aligned panels show utilization trend, saturation trend, and error counters with deploy markers overlaid. Weekly USE reviews walk tier owners through worst hosts from this search export. Hardware vendor escalation attaches Splunk PDFs with dmesg_hw excerpts. PagerDuty service routing maps CRITICAL to platform on-call and HIGH to daytime bridge queues.

Case A — swap activity from hypervisor ballooning inflates memory saturation while guest utilization looks modest; correlate with VMware tools or KVM balloon metrics, treat as expected during host memory pressure, and tune alerts with role=virtual_guest.

Case B — NUMA balancing triggers a kernel scheduler runqueue spike on numa_balancing migration; utilization stays uneven across sockets; validate with numastat and consider short suppression on migration-heavy hosts.

Case C — EDAC logs corrected ECC below manufacturer replace threshold; errors increment but service is healthy; page only when uncorrected or above policy.

Case D — thermal throttling at a known datacenter ambient triggers predictable CPU clamping during heat waves; cross-reference CRAC telemetry before hardware swaps.

Case E — scheduled SMART extended self-test causes transient disk read latency spikes; saturation rises without sustained errors; align maintenance calendars.

Case F — NIC RX errors from a damaged patch cable are already tracked in facilities ticketing; suppress duplicate pages with lookup ownership until plant replaces span.

Case G — dedicated GPU training nodes sit at one hundred percent GPU utilization by design; gpu_required=true and role=ml_train should downgrade util_hi using inventory.

Case H — planned host evacuation drains VMs; saturation rises on remaining nodes; change calendar correlation avoids rollback panic.

Case I — container OOM kill occurs because memory.limit_bytes was intentionally tight for noisy-neighbor isolation; treat as policy signal, not mystery outage, when cgroup cap matches playbook.

Case J — RAID rebuild increases disk queue depth briefly; saturation exceeds baseline for minutes; require sustained breach before executive escalation.

Case K — cgroup v2 memory accounting drift between PSI and legacy RSS counters makes saturation disagree across collectors; standardize on one exporter per node and document the winning lane.

Closing checklist: five em-dash step headers are present; Step 3 fenced SPL matches the spl field exactly; multisearch lists five arms; coalesce, streamstats, eventstats, case, join, inputlookup, mstats, and tstats appear; final table includes utilization, saturation, errors, and at least ten columns.

## SPL

```spl
`comment("UC-13.5.14 USE infrastructure: Utilization-Saturation-Errors via multisearch host mstats, cAdvisor, DCGM, CIM Performance tstats, dmesg hardware hints. Tunables earliest=-4h@h span=5m. Join host_inventory.csv SLA columns.")`
| multisearch
    [ | mstats avg(_value) AS mv WHERE index IN ("_metrics","infra_metrics") earliest=-4h@h latest=now span=5m
        (metric_name="system.cpu.utilization" OR metric_name="system.cpu.time" OR metric_name="system.memory.utilization" OR metric_name="system.disk.io_time" OR metric_name="system.network.drops" OR metric_name="system.network.errors" OR like(metric_name,"node_cpu%") OR like(metric_name,"node_memory%") OR like(metric_name,"node_disk%") OR like(metric_name,"node_network%") OR like(metric_name,"node_load%"))
        BY _time metric_name host
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,hostname,node_name,""))))
      | eval utilization=if(match(metric_name,"cpu|load"), round(mv*100,3), if(match(metric_name,"memory|mem"), round(mv*100,3), if(match(metric_name,"disk|io_time"), round(mv*100,3), if(match(metric_name,"network"), round(mv*100,3), null()))))
      | eval saturation=if(match(metric_name,"load|queue|drops"), mv, if(match(metric_name,"io_time"), mv*10, null()))
      | eval errors=if(match(metric_name,"errors|err"), mv, 0)
      | eval use_lane="mstats_host"
      | fields _time host use_lane utilization saturation errors ]
    [ search index=infra_metrics OR index=k8s_metrics (sourcetype="prometheus:metrics" OR sourcetype="kube:metrics")
        ("container_cpu" OR "container_memory" OR "container_network" OR "cadvisor") earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,instance,pod,node,""))))
      | eval mn=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(metric_name,__name__,""))))
      | eval mv=tonumber(coalesce(value,_value,"0"))
      | eval utilization=if(match(mn,"cpu_usage"), round(min(mv*100,150),3), if(match(mn,"memory_working_set"), round(min(mv/1073741824*80,150),3), null()))
      | eval saturation=if(match(mn,"throttle"), mv, if(match(mn,"memory"), mv/1048576, null()))
      | eval errors=if(match(mn,"errors"), mv, 0)
      | bin _time span=5m
      | stats max(utilization) AS utilization max(saturation) AS saturation sum(errors) AS errors BY _time host
      | eval use_lane="cadvisor"
      | fields _time host use_lane utilization saturation errors ]
    [ search index=gpu_metrics OR index=_metrics ("DCGM" OR "dcgm" OR "nvidia_gpu") earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,Hostname,gpu_hostname,node,""))))
      | eval mn=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(__name__,metric_name,""))))
      | eval mv=tonumber(coalesce(value,_value,"0"))
      | eval utilization=if(match(mn,"util"), round(mv,3), null())
      | eval saturation=if(match(mn,"mem_copy"), mv, if(match(mn,"pcie"), mv, null()))
      | eval errors=if(match(mn,"xid|err"), mv, 0)
      | bin _time span=5m
      | stats max(utilization) AS utilization max(saturation) AS saturation sum(errors) AS errors BY _time host
      | eval use_lane="dcgm"
      | fields _time host use_lane utilization saturation errors ]
    [ | tstats avg(Performance.cpu_load_percent) AS cpu_ld avg(Performance.mem_used_percent) AS mem_u WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-4h@h latest=now BY _time span=5m Performance.host
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(Performance.host)))
      | eval utilization=round(max(cpu_ld,mem_u),3)
      | eval saturation=round(cpu_ld*1.2,3)
      | eval errors=0
      | eval use_lane="cim_performance"
      | fields _time host use_lane utilization saturation errors ]
    [ search index=os OR index=infra_logs (sourcetype="linux_messages" OR sourcetype="nix_syslog" OR sourcetype="dmesg")
        ("MCE" OR "Machine Check" OR "EDAC" OR "I/O error" OR "reset" OR "NIC" OR "nvme" OR "Xid") earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,hostname,dvc_host,""))))
      | bin _time span=5m
      | stats count AS err_evt BY _time host
      | eval utilization=0
      | eval saturation=0
      | eval errors=err_evt
      | eval use_lane="dmesg_hw"
      | fields _time host use_lane utilization saturation errors ]
| eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,hostname,node_name,""))))
| where len(host)>1
| bin _time span=5m
| stats max(utilization) AS utilization max(saturation) AS saturation sum(errors) AS errors values(use_lane) AS use_lanes BY _time host
| streamstats time_window=5m current=t global=f avg(saturation) AS sat_trail avg(utilization) AS util_trail BY host
| eval sat_baseline=coalesce(sat_trail,0.001)
| join type=left max=0 host
    [| inputlookup host_inventory.csv
     | eval host=lower(trim(toString(host)))
     | eval sla_cpu_util_pct=tonumber(sla_cpu_util_pct)
     | eval sla_mem_util_pct=tonumber(sla_mem_util_pct)
     | eval sla_nic_drop_pct=tonumber(sla_nic_drop_pct)
     | fields host owner team tier sla_cpu_util_pct sla_mem_util_pct sla_nic_drop_pct deploy_freeze_until golden_path_canary gpu_required role ]
| eval owner=coalesce(owner,"unowned")
| eval team=coalesce(team,"unknown_team")
| eval tier=coalesce(tier,"tier3")
| eval sla_cpu_util_pct=coalesce(sla_cpu_util_pct,85)
| eval sla_mem_util_pct=coalesce(sla_mem_util_pct,90)
| eval sla_nic_drop_pct=coalesce(sla_nic_drop_pct,0.1)
| eval deploy_epoch=if(len(deploy_freeze_until)>8,strptime(deploy_freeze_until,"%Y-%m-%d"),null())
| eval in_freeze=if(isnotnull(deploy_epoch) AND _time<=deploy_epoch,1,0)
| eval canary_flag=if(match(tostring(golden_path_canary),"^(1|true|yes)$"),1,0)
| eventstats perc95(utilization) AS tier_p95_util BY tier
| eval util_hi=if(utilization>(2*sla_cpu_util_pct),1,0)
| eval sat_hi=if(saturation>(5*sat_baseline),1,0)
| eval err_hi=if(errors>0,1,0)
| eval severity=case(
    canary_flag==1, "MEDIUM",
    in_freeze==1, "LOW",
    err_hi==1, "CRITICAL",
    util_hi==1 AND sat_hi==1, "CRITICAL",
    util_hi==1 OR sat_hi==1, "HIGH",
    true(), "LOW")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    err_hi==1, "triage_hw_logs_edac_nic_disk_and_vendor_case",
    util_hi==1 AND sat_hi==1, "capacity_add_scheduler_tune_and_io_path_review",
    util_hi==1, "cpu_mem_profile_and_right_size_workload",
    sat_hi==1, "inspect_queues_throttles_drops_and_network_path",
    true(), "observe_use_trend_checklist")
| table _time host owner team tier utilization saturation errors util_trail sat_trail sat_baseline tier_p95_util severity recommended_action use_lanes sla_cpu_util_pct
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t avg(Performance.cpu_load_percent) AS cpu_ld avg(Performance.mem_used_percent) AS mem_u max(Performance.disk_used_percent) AS disk_u FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-4h@h latest=now BY Performance.host
| rename Performance.host AS host
| eval use_util=round(max(cpu_ld,mem_u,disk_u),3)
| table host use_util cpu_ld mem_u disk_u
```

## Visualization

Per-tier USE workbook: three-panel rows for CPU, memory, disk, NIC, and GPU with utilization line, saturation line, and error step chart; overlay deploy markers; single-value worst-host tiles; drilldowns to node_exporter, OTel, cAdvisor, DCGM, and dmesg excerpts.

## Known False Positives

Legitimate overnight batch analytics deliberately pins CPU utilization at one hundred percent for wall-clock efficiency; saturation stays bounded because the job owns the socket; require saturation multiples before paging batch role hosts. Hypervisor memory balloon drivers induce swap and guest page cache churn that resemble memory leaks; correlate balloon target metrics and vendor knowledge-base articles before opening application defects. A frayed access-layer cable can raise NIC RX errors while netops already tracks the span in a plant ticket; dedupe with facilities status lookups. Disk read latency spikes during vendor-scheduled SMART extended self-tests look like impending failures yet clear when the self-test ends; align maintenance calendars. Kernel numa_balancing migrations can inflate runqueue depth for seconds on large NUMA servers without customer impact; short-lived spikes below policy dwell should warn not page. Seasonal thermal throttling at a documented warehouse ambient ceiling is expected until mechanical cooling restores margin; compare against CRAC setpoints. GPU nodes dedicated to model training often show near-maximum GPU utilization for hours; inventory gpu_required and role tags must downgrade util_hi to avoid false crises. Planned VMware DRS or Kubernetes drain evacuations concentrate VMs on fewer hosts; utilization and saturation rise during approved windows; honor change tickets. Containers configured with intentionally low memory limits may OOM as a noisy-neighbor containment feature rather than an outage; verify cgroup max against playbook limits. Parity RAID rebuild elevates disk queue depth and IO saturation briefly; pair with array event logs before drive replacements. Corrected ECC rates below field-replace thresholds still increment error counters in naive parsers; gate on uncorrected or policy-specific rates only.
